  This is the upgrade plan template generated by the "Generate Upgrade Plan" agent.
  SESSION_ID should be replaced with the actual session id.

  ## PLANNING RULES

  !!! DON'T REMOVE THIS COMMENT BLOCK BEFORE FINAL PLAN IS GENERATED AS IT CONTAINS IMPORTANT INSTRUCTIONS.

  ### Strategy
  - **Incremental upgrades**: Use intermediates to avoid large jumps breaking builds
  - **Minimal changes**: Only upgrade dependencies essential for compatibility
  - **Risk-first**: Handle EOL/challenging deps early in isolated steps
  - **Necessary/Meaningful steps only**: Each step MUST change code/config. NO steps for pure analysis/validation. Merge small related changes.

  ### Intermediate Version Strategy
  Use intermediates when direct upgrade risks breaking builds. Good intermediate has:
  - **Stability**: Stable LTS release with production track record
  - **Compatibility bridge**: Bridges compatibility between current and target versions

  ### Mandatory Steps
  - **Step 1 (MANDATORY)**: Setup Environment - Install all JDKs/build tools marked `<TO_BE_INSTALLED>`
  - **Step 2 (MANDATORY)**: Setup Baseline - Run compile/test with current JDK, document results.
  - **Steps 3-N**: Upgrade steps - dependency order, high-risk early, isolated breaking changes
  - **Final step (MANDATORY)**: Final Validation - verify all goals met, all TODOs resolved, 100% tests pass (if tests enabled in Options)

  ### Verification Expectations
  - **Steps 1-N (Setup/Upgrade)**: Focus on COMPILATION SUCCESS. Tests may fail during intermediate steps.
  - **Final Validation**: COMPILATION SUCCESS + 100% TEST PASS (if tests enabled in Options)
  ### Efficiency (IMPORTANT)
  - **Targeted reads**: Use `grep` over full file reads; read specific sections, not entire files. Template files are large - only read the sections you need.
  - **Quiet commands**: Use `-q`, `--quiet` for build/test commands when appropriate
  - **Progressive writes**: Update plan.md incrementally, not at end

### Technology Stack

| Technology/Dependency     | Current | Min Compatible | Why Incompatible |
| ----------------------- | ------- | -------------- | --------------- |
| Java                     | 17      | 21             | User requested (17 LTS → 21 LTS) |
| Spring Boot              | 3.2.0   | 3.2.0          | Already supports Java 21 |
| Maven                    | 3.9.13  | 3.9+           | Fully supports Java 21 |
| maven-compiler-plugin    | 3.11.0  | 3.11.0         | Supports Java 21 (via Spring Boot BOM) |
| pdfbox                   | 3.0.0   | 3.0.0          | Java 21 compatible |
| opennlp-tools            | 2.3.0   | 2.3.0          | Java 21 compatible |
| jsoup                    | 1.17.2  | 1.17.2         | Java 21 compatible |
| postgresql               | Latest  | Latest         | Java 21 compatible |

### Derived Upgrades

**No derived upgrades needed.** Spring Boot 3.2.0 already supports Java 21 directly. All dependencies are compatible with Java 21. This is a direct JDK version upgrade without framework or dependency changes.

## Upgrade Steps

---

- **Step 1: Setup Environment**
  - **Rationale**: Ensure all required JDKs and build tools are available and verified before starting upgrade.
  - **Changes to Make**:
    - [ ] Verify JDK 17 is available (for baseline testing)
    - [ ] Verify JDK 21 is available and set as active
    - [ ] Verify Maven 3.9.13 is available and executable
  - **Verification**:
    - Command: Run `java -version` with JDK 21, expect: "openjdk version \"21.0.x\"" or similar
    - Verify Maven: `mvn --version` with JDK 21, expect: Maven 3.9.13 and Java 21
    - Expected: All tools verified and ready

---

- **Step 2: Setup Baseline**
  - **Rationale**: Establish pre-upgrade compile and test results as acceptance criteria.
  - **Changes to Make**:
    - [ ] Run baseline compilation with JDK 17
    - [ ] Run baseline tests with JDK 17
    - [ ] Document baseline test pass rate
  - **Verification**:
    - Command: `mvn clean test-compile` (with JDK 17)
    - Command: `mvn clean test` (with JDK 17)
    - JDK: 17.0.18
    - Expected: Document compilation SUCCESS and test results (forms baseline for comparison)

---

- **Step 3: Update pom.xml to Java 21**
  - **Rationale**: Core JDK version upgrade. All dependencies are already compatible with Java 21.
  - **Changes to Make**:
    - [ ] Update `<java.version>` from 17 to 21 in pom.xml
    - [ ] Update `<maven.compiler.source>` from 17 to 21 in pom.xml
    - [ ] Update `<maven.compiler.target>` from 17 to 21 in pom.xml
  - **Verification**:
    - Command: `mvn clean test-compile` (with JDK 21)
    - JDK: 21.0.9
    - Expected: Compilation SUCCESS (no errors)

---

- **Step 4: Final Validation**
  - **Rationale**: Verify all upgrade goals met. All tests must pass with Java 21 (100% pass rate or ≥ baseline).
  - **Prerequisites**: JDK 21 must be active
  - **Changes to Make**:
    - [ ] Verify all target versions are set to Java 21 in pom.xml
    - [ ] Run full test suite with JDK 21
    - [ ] Fix any test failures (iterative loop)
    - [ ] Verify no compilation warnings or errors
  - **Verification**:
    - Command: `mvn clean test` (with JDK 21)
    - JDK: 21.0.9
    - Expected: Compilation SUCCESS + 100% tests pass

## Key Challenges

**Minimal risk upgrade** — Java 17 → 21 is a straightforward LTS-to-LTS upgrade with full backward compatibility.

- **Virtual Threads (Preview feature)**: Java 21 includes Virtual Threads (Project Loom). No impact on existing code unless explicitly using Thread.startVirtualThread(). Existing thread pool patterns continue to work identically.
- **Build System Compatibility**: Maven 3.9.13 fully supports Java 21 compilation with current maven-compiler-plugin (3.11.0+, provided by Spring Boot 3.2.0 BOM). No issues expected.
- **Library Compatibility**: All dependencies (PDFBox 3.0.0, OpenNLP 2.3.0, jsoup 1.17.2, PostgreSQL driver) are confirmed Java 21 compatible. No library upgrades needed.

**Mitigation**:
- All changes are configuration-only (pom.xml java.version properties)
- Backward compatibility guaranteed by Java LTS policy
- Tests will validate runtime behavior
